contribution.title: Kerr solution
contribution.description: Exact axially symmetric vacuum solution to Einstein's field equations describing rotating black holes.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: The other foundational metric in this project. Used in tests/smoke_tests.cpp via kerr_isco_radius.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Algebraically special spacetimes (Petrov type D); guess-and-verify backed by symmetry.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What symmetries can I exploit to reduce the field equations?
What conserved quantities does the symmetry guarantee?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Ignoring the rich global structure of Kerr (multiple horizons, ergosphere, ring singularity, closed timelike curves inside the inner horizon).

reference.citation: Kerr, R. P. (1963). Gravitational field of a spinning mass as an example of algebraically special metrics. Phys. Rev. Lett. 11, 237.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Designing modules that touch rotating black holes, ISCO, photon sphere with spin, frame dragging, or the ergosphere.
how_to_apply.cautions: The maximally extended Kerr spacetime includes pathologies inside the inner horizon. Validation suites should use astrophysically reasonable parameter ranges.
